figures.caption: Figure 1: (a) Entropy-guided RL can leads to accuracy drop when prefering high entropy. (b) Unlike previous work, Policy Split innovatively bifurcates the policy into normal and high-entropy modes. (c) Through dualmode entropy regularization in Policy Split, two modes obtain a larger KL divergence and exhibit distinct entropy behaviors.

Table 5: The absolute difference between the high entropy mode and the original mode in average accuracy, average entropy, average response length, and the forward KL divergence between the two modes. The highest results for each model are shown in bold. The training process of Policy Split effectively enlarges the distance between the policies of the two modes.

Table 3: Ablation experiments of entropy coefficient for high entropy mode η, entropy coefficient for normal mode η′, and the entropy computation weight α. All experiments are based on Qwen3-1.7B, and the reported scores of accuracy and entropy are the average scores of normal mode across eight runs on all four tasks.
Table 7: Experiments of applying different modeswitching system prompts on Qwen3-8B-based models.
Figure 2: The training framework of Policy Split.
Table 1: Main results. The highest average results for each model are shown in bold. All improvement of Policy Split over original models have passed the significance test with p < 0.01.

Table 2: Experiments of creative writing on Qwen3-8B-based models. Metrics include rule-based ones (N-gram diversity and self-BLEU diversity) and LLM-as-a-Judge ones (Creativity, Originality, Narrative Flow, Emotional Impact, and Imagery). All scores are scaled at 100. The best results are shown in bold.

Table 10: Experiments of creative writing on Qwen3-8B-based models. Metrics include rule-based ones (N-gram and Self-BLEU) and LLM-as-a-Judge ones (Creativity, Originality, Narrative Flow, Emotional Impact, and Imagery).
